annotations.label: Definition of the Möbius function

anchor.snippet: The Möbius function is defined by

annotations.note: ArithmeticFunction.moebius is defined exactly as (-1)^cardFactors n for squarefree n and 0 otherwise.

anchors.snippet: The Möbius function is defined by

anchors.value: {\displaystyle \mu (n)={\begin{cases}1&{\text{if }}n=1\\(-1)^{k}&{\text{if }}n{\text{ is the product of }}k{\text{ distinct primes}}\\0&{\text{if }}n{\text{ is divisible by a square}}>1.\end{cases}}}

annotations.note: The Liouville function and the ω/Ω prime-counting functions exist, but the identity expressing μ in terms of them is not stated.

anchor.snippet: Another characterization by Carl Friedrich Gauss is the sum of all primitive roots

annotations.note: No declaration equating μ(n) with the sum of primitive n-th roots of unity was found.

annotations.note: LSeries_zeta_mul_Lseries_moebius proves L(ζ)·L(μ)=1 for re s>1, i.e. the Möbius L-series is 1/ζ(s).

annotations.note: The Euler product of ζ is formalized (riemannZeta_eulerProduct), but the reciprocal product ∏(1−p^{−s}) form for the Möbius series is not separately stated.

annotations.label: Möbius function is multiplicative

anchor.snippet: The Möbius function is multiplicative

annotations.note: isMultiplicative_moebius proves μ is multiplicative (IsMultiplicative μ).

annotations.note: moebius_mul_coe_zeta (μ*ζ=1) is exactly ∑_{d|n}μ(d)=[n=1] once unfolded via coe_mul_zeta_apply.

annotations.note: Möbius inversion and identities like ∏(1−f p)=∑μ(d)f(d) are formalized, but the specific list of further identities is only partially covered.

annotations.label: Binomial coefficients alternating-entry result

anchor.snippet: the binomial coefficients exhibit alternating entries of odd and even power

annotations.note: The binomial-coefficient combinatorial argument used in this Möbius proof is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Mean value is zero (equivalent to PNT)

anchor.snippet: The mean value (in the sense of average orders) of the Möbius function is zero

annotations.note: The PNT-equivalent statement that the mean value of μ is zero is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: μ(n)=0 iff divisible by square of a prime

anchor.snippet: is divisible by the square of a prime

annotations.note: moebius_ne_zero_iff_squarefree gives μ n≠0 ↔ Squarefree n, which with Nat.squarefree_iff_prime_squarefree yields μ n=0 ↔ a prime square divides n.

annotations.label: Möbius function of a locally finite poset

anchor.snippet: every locally finite partially ordered set (poset) is assigned an incidence algebra

annotations.note: IncidenceAlgebra.mu is the Möbius function on a locally finite poset, defined as the inverse of the zeta function in the incidence algebra.

annotations.note: The incidence/arithmetic-function convolution ring structure exists, but no statement about iterated Dirichlet convolutions of μ specifically is formalized.
